key_insight: TopoAgent turns multimodal scientific reasoning from a fragile linear workflow into a self-evolving DAG of isolated sub-tasks, improving robustness against context noise and planning errors.
engineering_relevance: For fracture and damage research, this offers a more reliable agentic framework for breaking complex inspection, simulation, or reasoning tasks into traceable, dependency-aware steps.
method: Front-end decomposer fractures a complex query into visually grounded atomic units.
Atoms are organized into a directed acyclic graph to enforce dependency-aware execution and strict context isolation.
Adaptive atomic fission splits bottleneck nodes at runtime when a tool cannot handle the current granularity.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive evaluation across mathematics, physics, and chemistry benchmarks, where TopoAgent outperforms state-of-the-art linear agent frameworks.
watch_item: Performance depends on correct decomposition and graph management, and runtime atomic splitting may add implementation complexity and overhead.
paper_title: TopoAgent: A Self-Evolving Topological Agent for Multimodal Scientific Reasoning
